title: neolacis pygmaea
intro: Neolacis pygmaea, also known as the dwarf lacis or miniature lacis, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the family Aizoaceae. It is known for its small, round rosettes and delicate white flowers. The medicinal properties of Neolacis pygmaea lie in its ability to soothe and calm the skin, making it an effective treatment for burns, wounds, and skin irritations. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as salves and creams to harness its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can promote wound healing and reduce the risk of infection.
